title: Specialist, Program Management (Program Management Specialist)
matched_skills: Fluent English and German (both explicitly required — met)
Data storytelling and translating data into solutions (C-level reporting, Power BI certified)
Project management experience (PMO at HSBC USD 8M programme, Project Coordination at Hapag-Lloyd)
Strong analytical skills (Power BI, KPI framework design, performance reporting)
Advanced Microsoft 365 / Excel / PowerPoint (PMO reporting and presentation work)
Cross-functional stakeholder coordination (6 workstreams at HSBC, global teams at Hapag-Lloyd)
missing_skills: 5+ years supply chain management experience (hard requirement — only ~1 year direct SCM at Hapag-Lloyd; total experience ~4 years across mixed domains)
Carrier allocation and forecast management (no direct experience with carrier procurement / capacity planning)
Client-facing program management in logistics (HSBC PMO was internal/digital transformation; Hapag-Lloyd exposure is partial)
key_requirements: Minimum 5 years supply chain management experience (Muss)
Strong analytical skills and ability to translate data into stories and solutions
Advanced Microsoft 365 including Excel and PowerPoint
core_responsibilities: Support and manage project development to improve APLL service efficiency
First point of contact for clients on process-related questions, issues, and support
Ensure strong client relationships through excellent service and continuous improvement
Communicate, train, and lead internal and external cross-functional teams globally
Collaborate with Key Account Management to identify process improvements and sales opportunities
Analyse data and present data analysis reports to clients (virtual and in-person)
Manage forecasts and carrier allocation in close collaboration with product teams, origin teams, and clients
cv_highlights: PMO at HSBC: USD 8M digital transformation programme, 6 workstreams, C-suite reporting, vendor management
Project Coordination at Hapag-Lloyd: agile, KPI tracking, stakeholder management in global shipping/logistics
Power BI Certified — data storytelling and performance reporting strength
Quadrilingual: English, German, Chinese, Cantonese — strong asset for global client-facing role
Multi-market coverage: EMEA and APAC exposure relevant to APL Logistics global footprint
gaps: Supply chain management experience is the critical gap — JD requires 5+ years; candidate has ~1 year directly in SCM (Hapag-Lloyd), with remaining experience in banking (HSBC), real estate (ECE), and marketing (IPC)
No carrier allocation or freight procurement experience
Limited direct client-facing programme management in a logistics context
recommended_emphasis: Lead with Hapag-Lloyd experience — position it as core supply chain / logistics programme management, emphasise any client-facing or cross-functional coordination achieved
Highlight data storytelling and reporting capability prominently — this is a direct match to the JD requirement of translating data into solutions for clients
Emphasise German + English fluency early — it is an explicit hard requirement (Muss) and a differentiator
Frame HSBC PMO as demonstrating programme governance maturity and cross-functional leadership at scale, to partially offset the SCM years gap
Mention Power BI certification and MS365 proficiency explicitly in the application
recommendation: Apply with tailored positioning. The supply chain years gap (5 required vs ~1 direct) is real and may screen out at HR level. However, the candidate's language match (German + English, Muss), logistics domain exposure (Hapag-Lloyd), data storytelling strength, and PMO credentials make this a viable stretch application. Recommended to tailor the cover letter to bridge the SCM gap by emphasising the breadth of logistics exposure at Hapag-Lloyd and the transferability of the HSBC programme governance experience.
